pages.text: $(o)"Transcendence can be channeled, never bottled — except in the right vessel."$()$(br2)The civilization's highest art is the $(l)Trinity$(): Ingot, Sword, Totem — all forged from $(l)Transcend Ingot$().

pages.title: Transcend Ingot
pages.text: The $(l)Transcend Ingot$() is the only material that stably contains raw Transcendence.$(br2)Its forging requires a $(l)Transcendence Core$() taken from an Ancient Warden — a primordial crystal capable of holding all eighteen elemental threads.$(br2)The ingot neither glows nor warms — but to touch it is to briefly $(l)see$() the edges of your own existence.

pages.title: Transcend Sword
pages.text: The $(l)Transcend Sword$() is the ingot's direct incarnation. It has two modes:$(br2)$(l)$(#fa0)Normal$()$() — cut matter$(br)$(l)$(4)Destruction$()$() — cut $(l)existence$()$(br2)Destruction was set by the ancient smiths as $(l)non-permanent$() — temporary only. Its bearers learn: $(o)"A power that can erase anything must eventually erase itself."$()

pages.title: Last Totem
pages.text: The $(l)Last Totem$() is the ingot's most merciful form. When the bearer dies, the totem takes the blow, erasing the law of "death" from that instant.$(br2)Cost: the totem is permanently consumed, and the bearer is left a $(l)blank memory$() — they will not remember they ever died.$(br2)$(o)"Death should not be an end — yet should not be forgotten. That is the totem's paradox."$()

pages.title: How the Three Relate
pages.text: Ingot is the $(l)vessel$(); Sword is $(l)active application$(); Totem is $(l)passive endurance$().$(br2)Together they form the civilization's complete philosophy of Transcendence: it may be carried, wielded, sheltered behind — never owned.$(br2)$(o)"We are only borrowers."$() — the joint epitaph of the trinity's smiths
